caption:
EID MILAD, MS. ADAWIYA A. ALAUYA (OIC-MUNICIPAL EDUCATION AND DEVELOPMENT OFFICE)!
Isang taos-pusong pagbati ng maligayang kaarawan mula sa Lokal na Pamahalaan ng Montalban. Ipinagdarasal namin ang patuloy na biyaya, kalakasan, at karunungan mula sa Poong Maykapal upang patuloy kang maging gabay sa iyong pangunguna at tapat na paglilingkod sa bayan.
Muli, Eid Milad mula sa Bayan ng Montalban!
#BayanNgMontalban
#MGADO

Ngayong araw, ginugunita natin ang Araw ng Kagitingan, isang pag-alala sa sakripisyo at pagmamahal sa bayan ng ating mga bayani, sundalo at bawat Pilipinong patuloy na lumalaban para sa bansa. 
Nawa’y magsilbi itong paalala na ang tunay na kagitingan ay makikita hindi lamang sa pakikipagdigmaan, kundi sa araw-araw na pagtindig para sa tama, pagkakaisa at malasakit sa kapwa.
Salute to those who fought before and those who continue to fight for our future. 
#ArawNgKagitingan
#LalawiganNgRizal 
#TaasNooRizalenyo

A safe community is a place where people feel protected, respected, and supported—physically, emotionally, and socially. It’s not just about the absence of danger, but also the presence of trust, inclusion, and care among members.
 Laws That Promote Safe Communities (Philippines)
Republic Act 9710 – protects women’s rights and safety
Republic Act 11313 – addresses harassment in public and online spaces
Republic Act 9262 – protects against abuse in relationships
A safe community starts with people looking out for each other. Even small actions—like kindness, awareness, and respect—can make a big difference.
Bagong Montalban
Montalban Gender and Development

𝐈𝐍𝐂𝐋𝐔𝐒𝐈𝐕𝐄 𝐆𝐑𝐎𝐖𝐓𝐇 𝐀𝐍𝐃 𝐃𝐄𝐕𝐄𝐋𝐎𝐏𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓 𝐊𝐍𝐎𝐖𝐒 𝐍𝐎 𝐒𝐓𝐄𝐑𝐄𝐎𝐓𝐘𝐏𝐄𝐒, 𝐁𝐈𝐀𝐒𝐄𝐒
The Department of Economy, Planning, and Development strongly supports initiatives that help in building a gender-inclusive workplace through conscious efforts in dismantling stereotypes and biases at their root. By eliminating sexist language from our official communications and normalizing gender-fair alternatives, we ensure every professional is seen and respected.
Here are some tips on how to use gender-fair language in the workplace:

𝗜𝗸𝗮𝘄 𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗻, 𝗷𝗼𝗸𝗲 𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗴 𝘆𝘂𝗻, 𝘄𝗮𝗹𝗮 𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗴 𝗺𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘀𝘆𝗮!
Violence isn’t always physical.
A "joke" isn't funny if it’s used to belittle, silence, or harass. Words can leave deep wounds, even without a single hand being raised.
Kaya let’s call out these “jokes” whenever we hear them and choose to build a world where respect is the priority.
Speak up. Let’s make safe spaces the standard, not the exception.
#PCW 
#VAWfreePH 
#SafeSpaces

𝗗𝗶 𝗸𝗮 𝗯𝗮 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘂𝗻𝗼𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗴𝗴𝗮𝗽 𝗻𝗴 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀?
Hindi lahat ng salita ay papuri. Kapag may halong kawalan ng galang, ito ay pang-aabuso at hindi isang “compliment.” True appreciation requires respect—and it shouldn’t feel degrading or unsafe.
Violence is often heard before it is felt. Verbal abuse diminishes dignity and creates an environment of fear. Kaya let’s refuse to stay silent and demand the safety every woman deserves.
Call it out. Let’s build a culture where “compliments” never come at the cost of respect.
#PCW 
#VAWfreePH 
#SafeSpaces

FIRST (1ST) QUARTERLY MEETING OF THE MUNICIPAL GAD FOCAL POINT SYSTEM (MGFPS)
Kaugnay ng patuloy na pagsusulong ng gender equality at pagpapatibay ng mga programang Gender and Development (GAD), isinagawa ngayong araw, Marso 30, 2026, ang 1st Quarterly Meeting ng Municipal GAD Focal Point System (MGFPS).
Pinangunahan ito ng Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) at dinaluhan ng mga kinatawan mula sa iba’t ibang tanggapan at departamento ng Pamahalaang Bayan ng Montalban. Tinalakay sa pulong ang mahahalagang usapin kabilang ang reconstitution ng MGFPS, GAD Center, budget allocation at utilization, pagsusumite ng mga ulat, at iba pang hakbang upang higit pang mapalakas ang implementasyon ng GAD programs sa komunidad.
Isinagawa ito sa patuloy na suporta ng Pamahalaang Bayan, sa pamumuno ni Mayor General Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret.).
#BayanNgMontalban 
#MGADO
